item.title: About
item.description:
I am a backend-focused developer who enjoys building practical software systems that solve real operational problems.

Most of my experience comes from developing management platforms, internal tools, and data-driven applications where reliability, structure, and maintainability matter.

I enjoy designing application logic, organizing data flows, and creating systems that remain understandable as requirements grow.

While I can work across the full stack when needed, my current focus is improving my backend engineering skills and exploring Go through hands-on projects.

item.title: E-Barker
item.description:
E-Barker is a web-based terminal management system designed to streamline the daily operations of public transportation terminals. It replaces manual passenger and trip monitoring with a centralized platform that enables dispatchers and terminal personnel to manage vehicle departures, monitor terminal activity, and maintain accurate operational records in real time.

The system provides administrators with a centralized dashboard for overseeing terminal operations, improving record accuracy, reducing paperwork, and supporting more efficient coordination between personnel. By digitizing traditional workflows, E-Barker helps modernize public transport terminal management while providing timely and organized access to operational data.

item.title: Expense Tracker API
item.description:
An expense tracking application built with Go that provides a RESTful API alongside a lightweight server-rendered web interface. Users can create accounts, securely authenticate, and manage their personal expenses while tracking their spending through a simple and responsive interface.

The project was built to explore modern backend development in Go, emphasizing clean architecture, type-safe database access, secure authentication, and minimal JavaScript by leveraging server-side rendering with HTMX.

item.title: Survey Profiling Tool
item.description:
A full-stack web application developed as a capstone project for the Archdiocese of Tuguegarao to digitize parish family profiling and survey management. The platform replaces paper-based data collection with a centralized system that enables families to submit profiling information online while providing parish administrators with tools to review, organize, and manage survey records efficiently.

Designed to support multiple parishes, the system improves data accessibility, streamlines administrative workflows, and provides a secure, role-based environment for managing household profiling information across the archdiocese.
